title: Open Problem (Bollobás, Leader, and Walters)
statement_text: Is it possible that $\mathrm{Val}_C < \mathrm{Val}_R$ for some game space $X$ and some $k$? In particular, is it possible that both players, the cops and the robber, win the game?
context_text: Bollobás, Leader, and Walters posed this as the main open problem in their influential work providing a general setup for pursuit-evasion games on metric spaces. The question asks whether a gap can exist between the guaranteed outcomes for the two sides, i.e., whether the game fails to have a min-max value.
paper_contribution: Answers the question in the negative for the geodesic-space version of the game: the paper's definition of the game and its approximation by finite discrete games yields $\mathrm{Val}_C = \mathrm{Val}_R$ (Theorem 12), establishing a min-max theorem.
notes: PDF source — statement reconstructed from prose description; no labelled theorem environment visible. Attribution year inferred from knowledge of Bollobás–Leader–Walters (GAFA 2012); not confirmed from bibliography in extracted text, which is truncated.
paper_title: Min-max theorem for the game of Cops and Robber on geodesic spaces
